entries.title: new freelancer posting random tips on tiktok
entries.body: I had just launched my web design business and had no idea how I was going to find clients. Zero interest in hunting them down. So I started posting random tips on TikTok, crossing my fingers someone would want to work with me.

entries.title: tiktok actually worked - leads in my inbox
entries.body: I would post a few videos and see leads coming into my inbox. This shit was fun. Built it to $10K/month from content alone, no outreach, no begging.

entries.title: follower count growing but income stayed the same
entries.body: I was capped at $10K/month and had no interest or energy to take on more clients. The audience was growing but the revenue wasn't going with it. I was on a treadmill.

entries.title: two years of trying everything else
entries.body: Testing day rates. Building a course. Flirting with digital products. I spent months building out a web design template with all the tutorials ready, only to post about it once and never talk about it again.

entries.title: completely burnt out - so i quit
entries.body: I stopped taking new clients. Scrapped my business. Started from scratch. This time, instead of bending over backwards trying to please the algorithms, I focused on simply serving my audience.

entries.title: six months of making zero money
entries.body: No income. No safety net. Just learning what my audience actually needed and testing simple products that solved real problems. It was the longest bet I'd ever placed on myself.

entries.title: broke and on the phone to my mum in tears
entries.body: I was completely broke. On the phone to my mum crying, ready to give up. That was the lowest point - six months of nothing and I had no proof this was going to work.

entries.title: first launch - $1,500 in 24 hours
entries.body: The next week after that phone call, I had my first successful launch. $1,500 in the first 24 hours. It wasn't life-changing money but it was proof the system worked.

entries.title: $41k in a single month
entries.body: February 2024: $41,202.87. From zero to consistent five-figure months in under six months. No team. No complex funnels. Just the right offer and a system that turns content into customers.

entries.title: i didn't need a bigger audience - i had enough
entries.body: The work was converting what I already had, not chasing more reach. I didn't need more consistency either. I needed a focused sprint and a system that fit my brain. The broken brain wasn't broken - it was just running someone else's playbook.

entries.title: first year on youtube: $320k in digital product sales
entries.body: $349K+ to date from digital products. The original videos kept selling for years - $20K/month on autopilot from videos I posted once. Long-form beats short-form when you're trying to sell.

entries.title: give the knowledge away free - sell the implementation
entries.body: Information is a commodity. Insight is rare. The most valuable thing I can do is give all the knowledge away and sell the implementation. That became the whole business model.

entries.title: building the solopreneur os in public
entries.body: Now I build a new feature inside the Solopreneur OS every week, document the build, and ship it as a YouTube video. The work is the proof. That's the flywheel.
